title: Correcting Invalid Git Tag Name Validation

statement.markdown: Write a function to validate a Git tag name. A valid Git tag name consists of [0-9a-zA-Z.-]+. However, it must not start with a dot or a hyphen. Additionally, it must not contain consecutive dots or hyphens. You should return True if the tag name is valid and False otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the Git tag name.
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the tag name is valid.
statement.notes: The tag name is case-sensitive and does not allow any other characters except the ones specified.

examples.explanation: The tag name 'v1.2' is valid because it does not start with a dot or a hyphen and does not contain consecutive dots or hyphens.
The tag name '.v1.2' is invalid because it starts with a dot.
The tag name 'v-1.2' is invalid because it starts with a hyphen.
The tag name 'v1.2.3' is invalid because it contains consecutive dots.
The tag name 'v1-2' is invalid because it contains consecutive hyphens.

hints.text: Use a regular expression to match the valid characters in the tag name.
Check if the tag name starts with a dot or a hyphen separately.

editorial.title: Correcting Invalid Git Tag Name Validation
editorial.intuition: To validate a Git tag name, we need to check if it matches the pattern [0-9a-zA-Z.-]+. However, we also need to ensure that it does not start with a dot or a hyphen and does not contain consecutive dots or hyphens.
editorial.bruteForce: One possible approach is to iterate over each character in the tag name and check if it is a valid character. If it is not, we can immediately return False. If we reach the end of the tag name, we can return True.
editorial.betterSolution: A better approach is to use a regular expression to match the valid characters in the tag name. We can also use string methods to check if the tag name starts with a dot or a hyphen and contains consecutive dots or hyphens.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a regular expression to match the valid characters in the tag name and then check if the tag name starts with a dot or a hyphen and contains consecutive dots or hyphens.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: We can prove the correctness of the solution by showing that it correctly handles all possible cases.
editorial.dryRun: Let's consider the tag name 'v1.2'. We can use the regular expression /[^0-9a-zA-Z.-]/ to match any invalid characters in the tag name. If the regular expression does not match any characters, we can return True. Otherwise, we can return False.
commonMistakes: Using a regular expression that matches too many characters
Not checking if the tag name starts with a dot or a hyphen separately
